reasoning: The baseline series value is 1.73% around late October 2025. Statistical trend analysis of the FRED historical data gives a 63% probability that the yield will be above this threshold by the November 2025 baseline, buoyed by seasonal analysis and mean reversion (since the yield had dropped from 2.34% in Jan to 1.73%). Recent October 2025 macroeconomic consensus from Reuters shows a median forecast for long Treasury yields to rise to 4.10% in three-to-six months as 'sticky inflation' and debt factors blunt the effect of recent Fed central rate cuts. This suggests upward pressure on real yields, though the inflation spread may consume part of the nominal yield increase. Following instructions to represent increasing uncertainty by moving probabilities closer to 0.5 for more distant horizons, the probability forecasts are stepped down for late November and January.
